claims.evidence_text: each variety has a maximum seed germination and vigor index under a specific temperature, with a required mean of 35 degree-days for visible germination to occur
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires a mean of about 35 degree-days for visible germination to occur.

claims.evidence_text: Wheat's booting and heading are important reproductive stages and are the most sensitive to drought, as heat and water stresses could lead to a drop of 30–90% in wheat grain yield
claims.claim_summary: Booting and heading are the wheat reproductive stages most sensitive to drought; heat and water stress at this period can drop grain yield 30-90%.

claims.evidence_text: The occurrence of water stress and high temperatures during a critical period of 2–3 weeks before anthesis can significantly impact the floret production, reducing the number of grains per spikelet, thus affecting the final yield
claims.claim_summary: Water/heat stress during the critical 2-3 weeks before anthesis significantly reduces floret production and grains per spikelet.

claims.evidence_text: due to the direct impact on grain number and dry matter accumulation, wheat reproductive and grain filling stages are the development periods most affected by high temperatures
claims.claim_summary: Wheat reproductive and grain-filling stages are the development periods most affected by high temperatures.

claims.evidence_text: while P application is automatically added at sowing (P applied as deep fertilizer), N application is prescribed before sowing, with three conventional applications: at sowing as deep fertilizer, at tillering stage (Z20 to Z25), and the start of the heading stage (Z50) as N-recovery fertilizer
claims.claim_summary: Conventional wheat N timing: three applications - at sowing (deep), at tillering (Z20-Z25), and start of heading (Z50) as N-recovery.

claims.evidence_text: the supply of N-fertilizer between stem elongation and the heading period amounts to 60% of the total N uptake and has a significant effect on increasing the number of spikelets and grains per ear, the grain protein content and grain weight, and causes an increase of 40% in grain yield
claims.claim_summary: N supplied between stem elongation and heading accounts for 60% of total N uptake and can increase grain yield by 40%.

claims.evidence_text: Parameter Timing of water stress Control Pre-anthesis Anthesis Grain filling LAI at booting 5.00 3.30 5.00 5.00
claims.claim_summary: Wheat LAI at booting was 5.00 under control conditions (reduced to 3.30 under pre-anthesis water stress).

claims.claim_summary: Wheat grains per spike was 32.7 under control conditions (dropping to 13 under pre-anthesis water stress).

claims.claim_summary: Wheat fertile tillers per m2 was 513 under control conditions across the water-stress experiment.
